title: Huawei Ascend 950PR Architecture Confirmed as DeepSeek V4 Foundation — Detailed Specs Published
detail: Industry analysts confirmed on April 5 that Huawei's Ascend 950PR — not the previously-reported 910C — is the specific chip architecture chosen by DeepSeek for its forthcoming V4 frontier model. TrendForce and DIGITIMES published detailed technical breakdowns: the Ascend 950PR delivers 1–2 petaflops of compute with an in-house HiBL memory stack of 112GB at 1.4 TB/s bandwidth, paired with a 2 TB/s inter-chip interconnect for cluster scaling. The chip includes Huawei's proprietary DaVinci AI compute cores optimized for mixture-of-experts transformer architectures — directly aligned with DeepSeek's V4 model design. The 950PR represents the top of Huawei's 2026 compute roadmap and is physically distinct from the 910C used in the Shenzhen 10,000-card cluster. Key industry implication: DeepSeek V4's adoption of the 950PR rather than 910C signals that China's frontier AI development is tracking Huawei's most advanced commercial silicon, not merely using readily-available supply. ByteDance, Alibaba, and Tencent have placed aggregate orders for hundreds of thousands of 950PR units with H2 2026 delivery, creating the demand signal needed to fund Huawei's next-generation chip R&D cycle.

title: China CAC Issues Draft Rules on Human-Like Interactive AI Services — Targets AI Companions and Emotional Chatbots
detail: China's Cyberspace Administration (CAC) released draft Interim Measures on Human-like Interactive AI Services for public comment (deadline May 6, 2026). The rules target AI systems that simulate human personalities, conduct emotional interactions, or form sustained simulated relationships with users — directly regulating AI companionship apps, customer service chatbots, and emotionally-intelligent AI assistants. Key provisions: (1) Providers must clearly disclose that users are interacting with AI at the start of every session and cannot disguise AI systems as humans; (2) AI services are prohibited from making false promises, developing 'excessive emotional dependence' in users, or engaging users in financial transactions under emotional manipulation; (3) Content must comply with 'core socialist values' and cannot endanger national security or social stability; (4) Age verification and parental controls required for services targeting users under 18; (5) Operators must maintain logs for six months and cooperate with government inspections. The draft rules target a rapidly growing domestic market — China has over 150 companies operating AI companion or emotional-support AI products, with combined daily active users exceeding 50 million. The regulations signal Beijing's intent to shape China's AI social layer around state-sanctioned norms, even as it accelerates AI capabilities investment elsewhere.
